todaysPlan.activity: Homework Reading – Erosion And Earth's Surface
todaysPlan.reason: Active uploaded homework is "Erosion and Earth's Surface Study Guide", so today's required work must anchor on erosion, landform, landforms, soil before broader profile goals.
todaysPlan.method: Read the assignment-aligned content, ask evidence-based checks, then use profile motivators only as framing.
probeSequence: Read or preview the assignment content
Ask why it happened using text evidence
Have the child explain the concept back

todaysPlan.reason: Check whether the child recognizes the core Erosion and Earth's Surface concepts before the teaching story.
todaysPlan.method: Measure: concept_recognition_accuracy; missed_concepts. Algorithm targets: retrieval-practice, reading-comprehension.

todaysPlan.reason: Build background knowledge and engagement around Erosion and Earth's Surface.
todaysPlan.method: Measure: reading_completion; hesitations; flagged_words. Algorithm targets: reading-comprehension, activity-affinity.

todaysPlan.reason: Academic words must be speakable before they can be explained confidently.
todaysPlan.method: Measure: pronunciation_accuracy; hesitation. Algorithm targets: pronunciation, desirable-difficulty.

todaysPlan.reason: Require active construction of the academic vocabulary needed to explain Erosion and Earth's Surface.
todaysPlan.method: Measure: construction_accuracy; attempted_value. Algorithm targets: retrieval-practice, desirable-difficulty.

todaysPlan.reason: Re-check Erosion and Earth's Surface concepts after intervention to decide whether to generate harder content.
todaysPlan.method: Measure: exit_accuracy; improvement_from_baseline. Algorithm targets: mastery-gating, retrieval-practice.

childProfile: Reina is an 8-year-old academic powerhouse—a year ahead of peers, competitive wrestler, bilingual English/Japanese speaker, and strategic thinker (chess, wrestling takedowns). She thrives on challenge, speed, and competition. She learns through application and competitive framing, not mechanical drill (worksheet coins crashed to 26% accuracy; game-based spelling hit 100%). She celebrates wins explicitly ('So much money, baby'), self-regulates beautifully (exits on winning streaks, requests harder material), and catches errors instantly (corrected Matilda mid-session multiple times). Wrestling metaphors land hard ('pin that answer'), Japanese celebration (すごい, やった) reinforces success, and Matilda movie references honor her chosen identity (smart girl who doesn't back down). She disengages from rote work and over-simplification but sustains focus when appropriately challenged. She requested multiplication/division 8 weeks ago and deserves to finally get it. Her reading fluency is championship-level but comprehension depth is untested. She's kind about mistakes ('Everyone makes mistakes') and gives meta-feedback on learning itself ('next time say it rhymes with computer'). Match her energy, honor her self-advocacy, and push her hard—she's built for it.
stopAfter: After the homework exit check shows the assignment concept is understood, or earlier if frustration is rising.
rewardPolicy: Reward only after the homework care-plan sequence, not before the assignment is checked.
